---
title: "Reproducible research"
author: "Miao Yu"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%d %B, %Y')`"
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE,eval = F)
```

# Database within rmwf package

```{r}
path <- system.file("extdata/pmd", package = "rmwf")
files <- list.files(path,recursive = T,full.names = T)

# pmd related dataset
# demo data from TBBPA https://pubs.acs.org/doi/abs/10.1021/acs.est.9b02122
tbbpa <- enviGCMS::getmzrtcsv(files[grepl('tbbpa',files)])
# demo data from in vivo SPME https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0003267018313047
MTBLS28pos <- enviGCMS::getmzrtcsv(files[grepl('MTBLS28pos',files)])
MTBLS28neg <- enviGCMS::getmzrtcsv(files[grepl('MTBLS28neg',files)])

# demo data from MTBLS341 https://www.ebi.ac.uk/metabolights/MTBLS341
MTBLS341exudatepos <- enviGCMS::getmzrtcsv(files[grepl('MTBLS341exudatepos',files)])
MTBLS341exudateneg <- enviGCMS::getmzrtcsv(files[grepl('MTBLS341exudateneg',files)])
MTBLS341leafpos <- enviGCMS::getmzrtcsv(files[grepl('MTBLS341leafpos',files)])
MTBLS341leafneg <- enviGCMS::getmzrtcsv(files[grepl('MTBLS341leafneg',files)])

path <- system.file("extdata/target", package = "rmwf")
files <- list.files(path,recursive = T,full.names = T)

# Targeted data
# demo data from MTBLS90 https://www.ebi.ac.uk/metabolights/MTBLS90
MTBLS90 <- enviGCMS::getmzrtcsv(files[grepl('MTBLS90mzrt',files)])
MTBLS90meta <- read.csv(files[grepl('MTBLS90meta',files)])

# demo data from MTBLS92 https://www.ebi.ac.uk/metabolights/MTBLS92
MTBLS92 <- enviGCMS::getmzrtcsv(files[grepl('MTBLS92mzrt',files)])
MTBLS92meta <- read.csv(files[grepl('MTBLS92meta',files)])

# demo data from ST001000 https://www.metabolomicsworkbench.org/data/DRCCMetadata.php?Mode=Study&StudyID=ST001000
ST001000_AN001878 <- enviGCMS::getmzrtcsv(files[grepl('ST001000_AN001878mzrt',files)])
ST001000_AN001879 <- enviGCMS::getmzrtcsv(files[grepl('ST001000_AN001879mzrt',files)])
ST001000_AN001880 <- enviGCMS::getmzrtcsv(files[grepl('ST001000_AN001880mzrt',files)])
ST001000_AN001881 <- enviGCMS::getmzrtcsv(files[grepl('ST001000_AN001881mzrt',files)])

ST001000_AN001878meta <- read.csv(files[grepl('ST001000_AN001878meta',files)])
ST001000_AN001879meta <- read.csv(files[grepl('ST001000_AN001879meta',files)])
ST001000_AN001880meta <- read.csv(files[grepl('ST001000_AN001880meta',files)])
ST001000_AN001881meta <- read.csv(files[grepl('ST001000_AN001881meta',files)])

# demo data from MTBLS404 https://www.ebi.ac.uk/metabolights/MTBLS404
MTBLS404 <- enviGCMS::getmzrtcsv(files[grepl('MTBLS404negmzrt',files)])
MTBLS404meta <- read.csv(files[grepl('MTBLS404negmeta',files)])

# demo data from MTBLS393 https://www.ebi.ac.uk/metabolights/MTBLS393
MTBLS393 <- enviGCMS::getmzrtcsv(files[grepl('MTBLS393mzrt',files)])
MTBLS393meta <- read.csv(files[grepl('MTBLS393meta',files)])

# demo data from MTBLS351 https://www.ebi.ac.uk/metabolights/MTBLS351
MTBLS351UPLC <- enviGCMS::getmzrtcsv(files[grepl('MTBLS351UPLCmzrt',files)])
MTBLS351UPLCmeta <- read.csv(files[grepl('MTBLS351UPLCmeta',files)])
MTBLS351GCxGC <- enviGCMS::getmzrtcsv(files[grepl('MTBLS351GCxGCmzrt',files)])
MTBLS351GCxGCmeta <- read.csv(files[grepl('MTBLS351GCxGCmeta',files)])

```

# Connection with other online database

## MetaboLights

```{r metabolights, eval=F}
# Use the MetaboLights REST API instead of the retired Risa workflow.
study_id <- 'MTBLS822'
study_url <- paste0(
  'https://www.ebi.ac.uk/metabolights/ws/studies/public/study/',
  study_id
)

table_to_df <- function(api_table) {
  field_order <- order(vapply(api_table$fields, `[[`, integer(1), 'index'))
  headers <- vapply(api_table$fields[field_order], `[[`, character(1), 'cleanHeader')
  out <- as.data.frame(do.call(rbind, api_table$data), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  colnames(out) <- headers
  out
}

study <- jsonlite::fromJSON(study_url, simplifyVector = FALSE)$content
metadata <- table_to_df(study$sampleTable)
assay_idx <- which(vapply(
  study$assays,
  function(x) grepl('fia_maf\\.tsv$', basename(x$metaboliteAssignment$metaboliteAssignmentFileName)),
  logical(1)
))[1]
assay <- study$assays[[assay_idx]]
assay_table <- table_to_df(assay$assayTable)

maf_file <- basename(assay$metaboliteAssignment$metaboliteAssignmentFileName)
maf_url <- paste0(
  'https://www.ebi.ac.uk/metabolights/ws/studies/',
  study_id,
  '/download/public?file=',
  utils::URLencode(maf_file, reserved = TRUE)
)
utils::download.file(maf_url, destfile = maf_file, mode = 'wb')
maf <- utils::read.delim(maf_file, check.names = FALSE)

sample_cols <- intersect(assay_table$`Sample Name`, colnames(maf))
data <- maf[, sample_cols, drop = FALSE]
group <- metadata[match(sample_cols, metadata$`Sample Name`), , drop = FALSE]
mz <- if ('mass_to_charge' %in% colnames(maf)) maf[['mass_to_charge']] else rep(NA_real_, nrow(maf))
rt <- if ('retention_time' %in% colnames(maf)) maf[['retention_time']] else rep(NA_real_, nrow(maf))

# ‚îÄ‚îÄ Optional: filter samples by metadata before constructing the list ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
# The sample table contains Characteristics and Factor Value columns returned
# by the API (e.g. 'Organism part', 'Timepoint', 'Sample type').
# Inspect what is available for this study:
print(colnames(group))   # e.g. "Sample Name", "Organism", "Organism part", "Timepoint" ...

# Keep only blood plasma samples at TimePoint 1 (adjust column/value as needed).
keep <- group$`Organism part` == 'blood plasma' & group$Timepoint == 'TimePoint 1'
data  <- data[, keep, drop = FALSE]
group <- group[keep, , drop = FALSE]

# construct the list
list <- list(
  data = data,
  group = group,
  mz = mz,
  rt = rt,
  feature_info = maf[, setdiff(colnames(maf), sample_cols), drop = FALSE]
)

# ‚îÄ‚îÄ Optional: download raw instrument files ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
# The assay table carries two file columns per row:
#   'Raw Spectral Data File'     ‚Äì original instrument file (e.g. .wiff, .raw, .mzML)
#   'Derived Spectral Data File' ‚Äì vendor-converted or centroided file
# Both use the same /download/public?file=<path> endpoint.
# WARNING: raw files can be several GB per study; filter to a subset if needed.

ml_download_files <- function(study_id, file_paths, dest_dir = study_id) {
  ml_base <- 'https://www.ebi.ac.uk/metabolights/ws/studies'
  dir.create(dest_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
  # remove empty / NA entries and deduplicate
  file_paths <- unique(file_paths[nzchar(file_paths) & !is.na(file_paths)])
  local_paths <- file.path(dest_dir, basename(file_paths))
  for (i in seq_along(file_paths)) {
    url <- paste0(ml_base, '/', study_id, '/download/public?file=',
                  utils::URLencode(file_paths[i], reserved = TRUE))
    utils::download.file(url, destfile = local_paths[i], mode = 'wb')
  }
  local_paths
}

# Download all raw files for the selected assay (can be large).
raw_files <- unique(assay_table[['Raw Spectral Data File']])
raw_local <- ml_download_files(study_id, raw_files, dest_dir = file.path(study_id, 'raw'))

# Download derived/converted files instead (often smaller mzML/mzXML).
derived_files <- unique(assay_table[['Derived Spectral Data File']])
derived_local <- ml_download_files(study_id, derived_files, dest_dir = file.path(study_id, 'derived'))
```

## Rmassbank

Workflow to process tandem MS files and build MassBank records.

```{r}
# https://bioconductor.org/packages/release/bioc/html/RMassBank.html
RmbDefaultSettings()
rmbo <- getOption("RMassBank")
rmbo$spectraList <- list(
    list(mode="CID", ces="10eV", ce="10eV", res=12000),
    list(mode="CID", ces="20eV", ce="20eV", res=12000)
)

rmbo$multiplicityFilter <- 1
rmbo$annotations$instrument <- "Bruker micrOTOFq"
rmbo$annotations$instrument_type <- "LC-ESI-QTOF"
rmbo$recalibrator$MS1 <- "recalibrate.identity"
rmbo$recalibrator$MS2 <- "recalibrate.identity"
options("RMassBank" = rmbo)

msmsList <- newMsmsWorkspace()

msmsList@files <- list.files(system.file("spectra.Glucolesquerellin",
                                         package = "RMassBankData"),
                             "Glucolesquerellin.*mzData", full.names=TRUE)

loadList(system.file("list/PlantDataset.csv",package="RMassBankData"))

Args <- list(method="centWave",
             peakwidth=c(5,12),
             prefilter=c(0,0),
             ppm=25, snthr=2)

msmsList <- msmsRead(msmsList, files= msmsList@files, 
                     readMethod = "xcms", mode = "mH", Args = Args, plots = TRUE)
msmsList <- msmsWorkflow(msmsList, steps=2:8,
                         mode="mH", readMethod="xcms")


mb <- newMbWorkspace(msmsList)
mb <- resetInfolists(mb)
mb <- loadInfolist(mb,system.file("infolists/PlantDataset.csv",
                                  package = "RMassBankData"))
## Step
mb <- mbWorkflow(mb, steps=1:8)
```


## Metabolomics WorkBench

```{r mwb, eval=F}
mw_base <- 'https://www.metabolomicsworkbench.org/rest'

# ‚îÄ‚îÄ Step 1: discover studies by project metadata ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
# Use the metstat endpoint to find studies matching species / sample source /
# disease criteria.  Any slot can be left blank; keep the semicolons.
# Format: <analysis_type>;<polarity>;<chromatography>;<species>;<sample_source>;<disease>
metstat_url <- paste0(mw_base, '/metstat/LCMS;;HILIC;Human;Blood;Diabetes')
hits <- jsonlite::fromJSON(metstat_url)
# hits contains study_id, analysis_id, and summary fields for each match.

# Alternatively, pull ALL study summaries and filter locally:
all_studies <- jsonlite::fromJSON(paste0(mw_base, '/study/study_id/ST/summary'))
# e.g. filter by species:
human_studies <- all_studies[grepl('Homo sapiens', all_studies$species, ignore.case = TRUE), ]

# ‚îÄ‚îÄ Step 2: batch-download targeted data by study ID ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
# rmwf::getmwlist() wraps /study/study_id/<ID>/metabolites
# rmwf::getmwfactor() wraps /study/study_id/<ID>/factors
target_ids <- c('ST000001', 'ST000553')
target_lists <- setNames(lapply(target_ids, rmwf::getmwlist), target_ids)
target_factors <- setNames(lapply(target_ids, rmwf::getmwfactor), target_ids)

# ‚îÄ‚îÄ Step 3: batch-download untargeted data by analysis ID ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
# /study/analysis_id/<AN>/untarg_data/  ‚Äì intensity matrix
# /study/analysis_id/<AN>/untarg_factors/ ‚Äì group info
get_mw_untargeted_api <- function(analysis_id) {
  data_url  <- paste0(mw_base, '/study/analysis_id/', analysis_id, '/untarg_data/')
  group_url <- paste0(mw_base, '/study/analysis_id/', analysis_id, '/untarg_factors/')
  raw   <- jsonlite::fromJSON(data_url)
  group <- jsonlite::fromJSON(group_url)
  list(
    data  = raw$data,
    mz    = as.numeric(raw$mz),
    rt    = as.numeric(raw$rt),
    group = group
  )
}

analysis_ids <- c('AN000113', 'AN000846')
untargeted_results <- setNames(lapply(analysis_ids, get_mw_untargeted_api), analysis_ids)

# ‚îÄ‚îÄ Step 4: filter samples by factor/metadata within a study ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
# untarg_factors returns one row per sample with all factor columns.
# Inspect available factor columns first:
res <- untargeted_results[['AN000113']]
print(colnames(res$group))   # e.g. "local_sample_id", "factors", ...

# Filter to a specific factor value ‚Äì adjust column name and value to match
# the factor columns returned for your study.
keep <- res$group$factors == 'Disease'   # example: keep only disease samples
data_filtered  <- res$data[, keep, drop = FALSE]
group_filtered <- res$group[keep, , drop = FALSE]

list <- list(
  data  = data_filtered,
  mz    = res$mz,
  rt    = res$rt,
  group = group_filtered
)
```

## rfigshare

```{r}
# Download demo data from figshare
library(rfigshare)
dir.create(file.path('data'), showWarnings = FALSE)
setwd(file.path('data'))
# NIST1950 postive data
dir.create(file.path('NIST1950'), showWarnings = FALSE)
setwd(file.path('NIST1950'))
article_id <- 11952558
details <- fs_details(article_id)
name <- sapply(details$files,function(x) x$name)
url <- sapply(details$files,function(x) x$download_url)
download.file(url, name)
setwd('..')
# matrix positive data
dir.create(file.path('Matrix'), showWarnings = FALSE)
setwd(file.path('Matrix'))
article_id <- 11952561
details <- fs_details(article_id)
name <- sapply(details$files,function(x) x$name)
url <- sapply(details$files,function(x) x$download_url)
download.file(url, name)
setwd('..')
path <- getwd()
files <- list.files(path,pattern = '*.mzML',recursive = T,full.names = T)
# DDA positive data
setwd('..')
dir.create(file.path('DDA'), showWarnings = FALSE)
setwd(file.path('DDA'))
article_id <- 11952555
details <- fs_details(article_id)
name <- sapply(details$files,function(x) x$name)
url <- sapply(details$files,function(x) x$download_url)
download.file(url, name)
path <- getwd()
ddafile <- list.files(path,pattern = '*.mzML',recursive = T,full.names = T)
# PMDDA positive data
setwd('..')
dir.create(file.path('PMDDA'), showWarnings = FALSE)
setwd(file.path('PMDDA'))
article_id <- 11952549
details <- fs_details(article_id)
name <- sapply(details$files,function(x) x$name)
url <- sapply(details$files,function(x) x$download_url)
download.file(url, name)
path <- getwd()
pmddafile <- list.files(path,pattern = '*.mzML',recursive = T,full.names = T)
```
